title: Unified Work Ledger Standard
purpose: Define the repo-native event-sourced work ledger for Type A agents: append-only raw substrate, deterministic derived projections, compact work-memory items, recipe-based retrieval, and runtime-enforced participation through host lifecycle hooks.
statement: The work ledger is raw_seed applied to agent work-voice: open work, progress, closure, supersession, and reopening are lifecycle events over one durable thread substrate, not separate files or mutable rows.

forward_integration_contract.rule: Work Ledger coordination is load-bearing when claims collide or read receipts are required, but a dirty tree by itself is not a blocker. Runtime policy classifies dirty surfaces, reserves destructive_override_required for loss-risk actions, and keeps claim/session closeout obligations visible without turning advisory dirt into a global stop sign.
destructive_boundary: delete ignored dependency installs, render receipts, or runtime caches outside the owning cleanup lane
regenerate over dirty projection without owner-tool or adoption receipt
closeout_order: Task Ledger sign-off or residual capture
Work Ledger closeout append while read receipt is live
session-finalize last; it refuses touched/no-append sessions by default and releases active claims only after the append exists

id_contract.rule: Thread ids are minted once and never reused. todo_supersede is the only lifecycle operation that mints a successor thread id. Reopen reuses the same td_*. Claim ids (wlc_*), pass ids (wlp_*), and sweep event ids (wls_*) are runtime-only — they live in state/work_ledger/runtime_status.json, never in the append-only JSONL substrate.

todo_open.semantics: Open a new work thread interval.

progress_note.semantics: Append a non-closing note to an existing thread.

todo_close.semantics: Invalidate the currently open interval on the same td_* and record explicit resolution provenance.

todo_supersede.semantics: Mint a successor td_* and preserve an explicit old->new supersession edge while resolving the prior thread with provenance.

todo_reopen.semantics: Reactivate a previously closed td_* by opening a new valid interval on the same thread id.

event_metadata_contract.purpose: Optional structured context for work rows whose source is another substrate or coordination surface. Metadata is additive and backwards-compatible; old rows without metadata remain valid.
rules: metadata.source_substrate names the upstream evidence plane such as agent_seed, raw_seed, bridge, metabolism, or host_surface.
metadata.source_paragraph_id must carry the exact par_agent_* / par_* id when a row is promoted from a seed paragraph.
metadata.dedupe_key is stable enough for importers to avoid opening duplicate work rows.
metadata is projected onto thread cards and work_memory_items, but it never replaces evidence_refs.

bi_temporal_semantics.created_at: When the event row was appended.
bi_temporal_semantics.valid_at: When the represented work claim becomes valid for its current interval.
bi_temporal_semantics.invalid_at: When the represented interval is invalidated or resolved. Null while open.
bi_temporal_semantics.expired_at: Optional time-bounded expiry marker; null in v1 unless an external policy sets it.

resolution_episode_contract.rule: todo_close and todo_supersede require a structured resolution episode. Bare 'done' strings are invalid.

projection_contract.rule: The projection is deterministic and rebuildable from raw JSONL. UI and API surfaces read projections; they do not own ledger business logic. work_memory_items are derived recall cards over ledger events, not an independent memory store.
projection_contract.mixed_phase_family_rule: When a raw phase bucket contains events for more than one family_id, the first observed family keeps the legacy work_ledger_index.json path and secondary families use work_ledger_index.<family_id>.json. A single generated index file must never be expected to be fresh for two different family-scoped source reductions at once.

runtime_status_contract.rule: The runtime status keeps the full session map for machines and also projects a compact cohort_overview for Type A agents coordinating concurrent autonomous seeds. Agent-facing lifecycle/status CLI commands must emit compact projections by default; the full sessions map is a diagnostic escape hatch only through explicit --full flags. The overview must preserve active sessions, effective active sessions, orphaned active sessions, stale sessions, actor/phase buckets, touched-thread contention, unknown-scope active sessions, unclaimed touched-work sessions, active claims, td_* claim collisions, path/scope claim collisions, recent sweep events, host-observed external sessions, pass-level awareness cards, heartbeat participation summary, and recommended coordination actions. Orphaned active sessions remain visible as stale coordination obligations, but they do not contribute to touched-thread, unknown-scope, unclaimed-touched, or claim-collision live contention.

pass_heartbeat_contract.purpose: Runtime-only pass-level workspace awareness: a bounded public now/done strip for live Type A coordination without raw transcript reading or hidden-reasoning extraction.
pass_heartbeat_contract.command: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/work_ledger.py session-heartbeat --session-id <id> --state <state> --now '<public current pass>' [--done '<public previous result>'] [--scope-ref <ref>] [--clip-lines]

compatibility_aliases.rule: Canonical recipes should keep session-heartbeat, --state, and --now/--done. The host CLI still accepts common closeout aliases at the parse boundary and writes only canonical runtime pass_state values.

line_limits.clip_lines_flag: --clip-lines keeps strict rejection as the default but lets session-heartbeat callers trim explicit public now/done text to the heartbeat limits before runtime validation.
line_limits.preflight_clip_lines_flag: --heartbeat-clip-lines applies the same opt-in trim at session-preflight and Work Landing begin ingress.

projection_rules: session-status --overview --cards-only must include awareness_cards with session id, actor, phase, freshness, current_pass_line, last_pass_result_line, pass state, source, scope refs, and claim refs; full per-card repair rows must collapse to repair_summary plus drilldown, while aggregate repair_rows keep the actionable commands.

session-status --overview --cards-only must include cohort_speed_summary with effective active session count, active claim count, active claim session ids, heartbeat participation counts, orphan count, and the claims/heartbeat fast paths for parallel Type A seed coordination.

session-status --seed-speed is the first-contact active-seed speed packet. It must omit awareness_cards, monitor_cards, repair_rows, recommended_actions, and full claim/session rows while preserving active claim-session cards, heartbeat counts, contention scalars, first_action, and drilldowns to cards/full evidence.

session-status overview must include heartbeat_participation with effective active session count, explicit current-pass count, projected_unknown count, participation ratio, first-contact ordering evidence, source/freshness counts, and the no-transcript-summarization policy.
First-contact cards must prioritize explicit live manual_cli/hook current_pass_line heartbeats over source=projected_unknown imports before applying compact output limits.
Sessions without an explicit heartbeat must project source=projected_unknown and current_pass_line=null rather than mining raw transcripts.

Imported external-observed sessions with no active claims, touched work/work ids, open todos, writes, live explicit heartbeat, ledger append/exemption, or observed mutation paths are passive observations: keep them visible as active sessions but exclude them from effective_active_sessions, heartbeat participation, mission-focus pressure, and multiple-active-session watch signals.
Orphaned or expired heartbeat rows must remain visible but demoted through freshness_state so stale sessions cannot masquerade as live current work.
Heartbeat updates are runtime coordination state, not durable task truth and not a replacement for Work Ledger progress/closeout appends.

participation_contract.rule: Participating sessions that can write should publish explicit public now/done pass lines at meaningful pass boundaries. Effective active means claim-bearing, touched-work, write-observed, or explicitly heartbeating coordination participants, not every imported host observation. Imported or read-only sessions may truthfully remain source=projected_unknown; the failure mode is participating writable sessions staying unknown or explicit heartbeats being hidden behind unknown imports.

session_yield_coordination_request_contr.purpose: Turn a non-destructive session-yield request into a paste-ready sibling-thread coordination brief with request id, requester, target session, blocked condition, held paths, do-not-touch boundaries, and owner acknowledgement template.
rules: The coordination request is generated from the yield request receipt; chat prose must not be the only authority that a sibling lane was asked to land, release, cede, or respond.
The brief is owner-visible, non-destructive, and append-only when the parent session-yield-request is written.
The generated message must include the yield request id so the owner can close it with session-yield-result.
The generated message must preserve sibling do-not-touch paths or sessions separately from the target owner's held paths.
session-status --seed-speed coordination packets should advertise a placeholder coordination-brief command so agents discover the brief lane before improvising prose.

shared_substrate_contention_envelope_con.purpose: When a mutation or exclusive claim is blocked by a live owner claim, emit the existing owner surface and legal coordination lanes instead of letting agents stop at prose like 'I did not edit because it is claimed.'
rules: A live claim blocks unsafe mutation; it must not block read-only owner-session inspection or append-only coordination requests.
The envelope must group collisions by owner session, preserve held surfaces, claim ids, lease maxima, and the requested surface that collided.

The first response must route through existing surfaces: read the owner session, send a coordination brief, claim a disjoint sibling surface, or capture a blocked residual only when coordination cannot proceed.
The failure-response floor is explicit: user-facing closeout must name the owner session, held surface, lease or freshness evidence, blocking condition, coordination command, and re-entry condition.
The envelope is generic over local examples such as package-data or pyproject companion surfaces; do not encode batch-specific names in the contract.

pre_mutation_route_selector_contract.purpose: Make mutation-check classify edit authority before the first write, so a clear foreign-collision scan cannot be mistaken for an unclaimed mutation lane.
rules: A mutation-check status of clear only means no active foreign owner claim collided with the requested scope; direct mutation is allowed only when pre_mutation_route_selector.mutation_allowed_now is true.
Without a session id, clear scopes route to session-preflight before mutation so the actor creates a read receipt and a forward-looking claim.
With a session id but no same-session active path claim covering the requested paths, clear scopes route to session-preflight or claim-current-session before mutation.
With a same-session active covering claim and no foreign/source-input collision, the legal decision is same_owner_continuation.

Foreign path collisions and source-input collisions under --require-exclusive route through the contention envelope, coordination brief, disjoint lane, or blocked receipt; they are never permission to clone the surface or mutate unclaimed.

Source-coupled write profiles must still prove source inputs are committed, same-session-owned, or clean-snapshot documented before landing generated outputs even when the pre-mutation route selector is otherwise clear.

observed_path_overlap_boundary_contract.purpose: Distinguish session-preflight observed_path_overlaps telemetry from live owner-claim blockers so agents do not downshift or stop when exact mutation claims are clear.
applies_when: session-preflight reports observed_path_overlaps for a requested path.
mutation-check or claim cards report no live same-path owner collision for the requested mutation.
Observed rows are imported host/session evidence, referenced-path evidence, or mutation_path_count=0 evidence rather than a claimed write owner.
rules: observed_path_overlaps are coordination telemetry and rediscovery evidence by default; they are not blockers without a live claim collision, active owner lease on the exact surface, or observed mutation evidence that changes the same path.

When observed rows have mutation_path_count=0 or only referenced_paths, and exact mutation-check/session-claims are clear, proceed through the claimed scoped mutation lane and record the overlap classification receipt.

Treat an observed overlap as blocking only when it resolves to an active owner claim, an unresolved same-path mutation session, or a mission/scoped preflight gate that explicitly refuses the current session.
If the overlap is watch-only but suggests high concurrency, keep the claim narrow, validate through owner surfaces, and avoid broad staging or projection refreshes outside the claimed path set.

blocked_primary_ambition_preservation_co.purpose: Preserve the user's or trace-selected primary capability target when an active claim blocks the exact mutation lane, so agents do not satisfy a high-value continuation request with an unrelated safe hygiene edit.
applies_when: A trace-summary, CAP, WorkItem, phase wave, or latest user intent selects a primary mutation lane.
mutation-check, session-preflight, session-claim-path, mission_transaction_preflight, or scoped_commit reports a live owner claim or claim-pressure blocker for the exact primary surface.
A smaller unclaimed edit is available but does not carry the same capability target, unblock value, or owner-surface lesson.
rules: The blocking claim is a flow-control signal, not a scope-reduction license.
A disjoint sibling edit is valid only when it advances the same capability target, route rule, validation/readback surface, or owner lesson selected by the blocked primary lane.
If the agent has a coherent candidate patch or plan for the blocked surface, preserve it through the claim-settlement continuation lane rather than keeping it only in chat.
If coordination is possible, use the contention envelope's owner session and coordination brief before declaring the lane blocked.
If switching to an independent WorkItem, record why the primary ambition has been preserved and why the independent item outranks waiting, coordination, or claim-settlement continuation.
Never close a trace/CAP refinement pass as successful with only a claim classification, CAP capture, ledger settlement, or prose summary when a safe owner-surface mutation is still available.
blocked_primary_ambition_preservation_co.failure_response_floor: Closeout must name the blocked primary surface, owner session or collision evidence, chosen legal continuation, re-entry condition, and validation/readback receipt. 'I chose a smaller safe edit because the main path was claimed' is invalid unless the receipt proves same-capability value or an independent WorkItem rank.

generated_projection_scope_receipt_contr.purpose: Separate a scoped agent-owned mutation from wider generated/source drift exposed by the owner builder, so agents do not stage foreign generated output or dismiss legitimate generated refreshes as noise.
applies_when: A builder, projector, scaffold, public mirror refresh, corpus generator, or --write command changes paths outside the declared owned slice.
A focused validation lane passes while the full generated write set includes unrelated source registries, manifests, projections, or untracked generated files.
The source authority inputs for generated output are dirty, claimed, stale, or owned by another live session.
rules: The command that produced a generated diff is not itself ownership proof for every generated path.
If generated outputs depend on dirty or claimed source authority, do not stage those outputs as the current mission unless the current mission also owns and validates the source authority inputs.

If source authority inputs are committed or owned, the write profile is declared, and full validation passes, the generated set may be landed as an intentional generated refresh with the source/input proof attached.

When the owned slice is valid but the wide generated set is foreign, land only the owned source/projection slice when separable and capture or record a claim-settlement continuation for the wide drift.
When the builder lacks a narrow dry-run/write route and the wide diff is not landable, record the missing write-profile or owner-route gap instead of hand-editing generated files.
Product, phase, organ, or corpus names are evidence labels, not the general rule; the receipt is generic over generated projection owners.

claim_settlement_continuation_contract.purpose: When a Type A lane is blocked by a live owner claim after preparing a coherent candidate delta, preserve that candidate as a durable, drainable continuation instead of ending at yield prose.
lifecycle: record_candidate when the blocked lane has a coherent patch, generated-output refresh, command plan, or review-only continuation
a live owner claim blocks direct mutation, not proposal authoring; record pending_diff when the patch can be named with base/path/touchpoint evidence

build_signoff_context or the context command refreshes active-claim evidence and attaches current worktree diffs, mechanical patch applicability checks, path commits since the pending diff base revision with a query/fallback receipt, path timestamps, active-claim review rows, and other pending diffs from the continuation queue before signoff
assess_candidate_payload or the assess command is the non-mutating signoff surface for pending-diff touchpoint interference before queue drain or approval
record_signoff or the signoff command appends an approve, reject, or needs_refresh decision into pending_diff.signoffs with reviewer provenance, context digest, evidence refs, and proof
watch active claims through state/work_ledger/active_claims_snapshot.json rather than keeping a reasoning thread alive to poll

drain only after rechecking claim state; executor/adjudicator apply paths require latest recorded signoff decision approve, while reject or needs_refresh signoffs append terminal proof without invoking the executor

fresh adjudicator must inspect signoff_context current worktree diffs, base-relative recent diffs plus query/fallback receipt, timestamps/thread provenance, active claims, other pending diffs, generated/source relationships, and validation receipts before approving
fresh adjudicator must classify clean_apply, adapt, supersede, reblock, or unsafe before mutation
when the current tree already contains the effective pending diff, mark already_satisfied with no-op proof instead of reopening a residual
all attempts append proof and a terminal class; no-effect closeouts are classified as no_effect_bug
guardrails: A continuation is not authority to apply blindly; the fresh adjudicator must re-read live claims, HEAD/CAS, current file content, source refs, and validations.
Drain must refresh active claims from Work Ledger runtime status before adjudication unless the caller explicitly supplies a snapshot file or opts into stale-snapshot mode.
Signoff decisions are append-only queue events; a later signer can approve, reject, or request refresh without rewriting the candidate body or prior signer proof.
A released pending diff with no recorded signoff waits in waiting_signoff and must not invoke an executor that could apply or adapt the candidate.

The default signer bias is to approve clean non-interference, but only after reviewing the pending diff's current worktree diff, mechanical patch applicability, base-relative recent-diff window, path/touchpoint overlap, thread provenance, active claims, generated-source relationships, and validation receipts.

signoff_context.other_pending_diffs must be hydrated from the continuation queue for open path-overlapping candidates; static placeholders, self-candidates, closed candidates, and non-overlapping candidates do not satisfy the signer context.

The touchpoint-interference evaluator must classify comparable disjoint same-file edits as signable, overlapping hunk/symbol/selector/generated-source touchpoints as conflict_pending_resolution, and stale-base/no-anchor cases as needs_refresh.
Do not use claim-settlement continuation for publication/push divergence unless the predicate is explicitly changed to publication reconciliation.
Do not apply generated outputs while their source refs are still actively claimed.
Do not use Microcosm-specific paths as fixture authority; tests must use synthetic paths.

external_session_observation_contract.purpose: Runtime-only parity lane for agents whose host surface is visible but whose process cannot yet call the hook-backed SessionStart bootstrap path. Initial use: local Codex threads from ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite.
external_session_observation_contract.session_id_shape: codex:<thread_id> for imported Codex host threads
rules: The importer opens Codex SQLite read-only and never writes into ~/.codex.
Imported sessions set external_observed=true, preserve external metadata, and carry no read_receipt_id; they make runtime coordination visible but do not bypass receipt-gated ledger writes.

Imported sessions without active claims, touched work/work ids, open todos, writes, live explicit heartbeat, ledger append/exemption, or observed mutation paths are passive host observations; they remain visible in active_sessions but do not create write-active coordination pressure.

session-import-host-surfaces imports both Codex threads and Claude IDE lock observations; Claude lock imports strip secrets such as authToken and preserve only coordination metadata (pid, workspace folders, IDE name, transport, todo counts).

When rollout_path is available, the importer tails the Codex rollout read-only and records a bounded codex_rollout_activity_summary_v1 in external_metadata.rollout_activity with recent tool names, command lines, referenced repo paths, and definite apply_patch mutation paths.
The full rollout_activity object stays on the per-session runtime row; compact cohort_overview rows must carry only a bounded digest so --overview remains usable during multi-agent bursts.
Rollout-derived paths are observations only; they must not create or imply session-claim / session-claim-path leases without an explicit claim command.
A fresh host update reopens or refreshes the runtime-only session; stale imported sessions age through the same orphan-display and orphan-sweep policy as hook-backed sessions.

session_preflight_contract.purpose: One-command manual lifecycle entry for Codex/Cursor/manual agents until host hooks exist.
sequence: Optionally import recent Codex host threads as runtime-only external observations.
Bootstrap the current session and mint a read_receipt_id.
Expand named write profiles into their known generated write sets before claiming paths.
Claim requested td_* and/or path scopes using the same wlc_* claim lifecycle.
Compare requested path scopes against rollout-derived recent mutation/reference paths from imported Codex peers and surface observed_path_overlaps.
Scan imported Codex rollout commands for shared-worktree git risks through the shared detector used by ./repo-git and surface shared_worktree_git_risks.
Return a compact preflight payload by default: read_receipt_id, import summary, claim summary, compact cohort risk/counts, and explicit closeout commands.
When --full is passed, return the full bootstrap object, imported candidates/observations, full claim rows, and full cohort lists for diagnostics.

raw_seed_family_projection: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed.json
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed.md
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed.snapshot.md
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed
agent_seed_family_projection: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/agent_seed.json
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/agent_seed.md
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/agent_seed.snapshot.md

source_input_claim_policy.purpose: Expose source-coupled builder input fences separately from generated output claims, so agents do not land generated projections that read another session's dirty or claimed source inputs.
source_input_claim_policy.source_coupled_profile_rule: If a selected write profile declares source_input_paths, mutation-check and session-preflight must surface active source-input claim collisions; with --require-exclusive they block before claims are written.
source_input_claim_policy.landing_rule: Generated outputs from a source-coupled profile may be staged only when source inputs are committed, owned by the same session, or rebuilt from a documented clean snapshot; otherwise record a claim-settlement continuation or blocked residual.

rules: Preflight never bypasses read_receipt validation; it only returns the receipt minted by bootstrap_session.

Preflight admission status is claim-authority gating. Agents must read the top-level `status`, `work_admission.allow`, and `read_receipt_id` before treating `claim_summary` or `claims` as live proof. If `status` is `blocked_by_work_admission`, `work_admission.allow` is false, or `read_receipt_id` is null, no session bootstrap or claims were written; `claim_summary.requested` / `claim_summary.refused` is a refusal report, not evidence that claims were retained and then disappeared. Use `--host-pressure-policy=warn` / `off` only as an intentional override, wait or relieve host pressure, or switch to exact lightweight `--path` claims when the work is genuinely a small source edit rather than a heavy write-profile rebuild.
Preflight claims are normal claims and therefore can be soft collisions or --require-exclusive refusals.

Write profiles are claim expansion macros for known generated outputs plus optional source-input fences for source-coupled builders; they do not run the generator and they do not bypass normal path-claim collision policy.

For source-coupled write profiles, output claims alone are not enough to land generated artifacts. The source_input_paths must be clean/committed, owned by the same session, or explicitly excluded through a clean-snapshot receipt before generated outputs can be treated as mission-owned.

task_ledger and autonomous_seed are broad coordination macros for shared event/projection/seed-loop surfaces; agents touching one specific seed or one exact non-generated path may still use explicit --path claims to avoid unnecessary sibling collisions.

observed_path_overlaps are advisory signals from imported host telemetry; they never create or imply a claim lease. Resolve them through observed_path_overlap_boundary_contract: referenced-only or mutation_path_count=0 rows with no active same-path claim are watch telemetry, while active owner claims or same-path mutation evidence require coordination, blocking, or lane switching.

observed_path_overlaps identity fields must not carry unbounded prompt, packet, or title bodies. Long session titles must be projected as bounded handles with title_preview, title_bytes, title_hash, title_kind, title_full_omitted, title_ref or drilldown, and an omission_receipt; the full source remains recoverable by rerunning the same session-preflight with --full instead of repeating per overlap row.

shared_worktree_git_risks are advisory signals for commands such as git stash, git reset --hard, git checkout --, git restore, and git clean -fd*. In a dirty multi-agent worktree, broad stash/apply/pop or restore/reset operations are unsafe unless every affected path is intentionally claimed and restored path-specifically.

mutation-check performs the read-only subset of write-profile/path collision detection, emits pre_mutation_route_selector, and returns non-zero when --require-exclusive would block mutation; when --session-id is provided, that session's own active claims are ignored as blockers but are still reported as same-session edit authority.

Scoped commit and mission landing must bind claim proof to the current owner session. Broad write-profile or directory claims are startup coordination evidence for overlapping child paths, but a landing gate may require exact owned paths or a session-bound overlap proof; if duplicate-claim dedupe collapses exact child claims into a broad parent and exact coverage is questioned, rerun the landing preflight with --session-id auto or release/narrow to exact child path claims before committing.

In dirty or concurrent trees, scoped landing should prefer scoped_commit.py private-index modes with --work-ledger-session-id over manual shared-index staging. Manual shared-index staging is permitted only as one contiguous stage, inspect cached names, commit critical section for exact owner paths; if a sibling commit consumes the staged owner paths, bind closeout to that landed commit, capture the coordination failure, and repair the owner rule rather than manufacturing an empty duplicate commit.

scoped_commit.py closeout hints must not present Work Landing reconcile as unconditional after-commit closeout. Reconcile is for sessions that established a Work Landing begin/attempt binding before mutation; direct scoped commits with no attempt binding must surface the append-exempt session-finalize template, read-receipt placeholder, commit ref, and do-not-follow-with-bare-finalize rule.

When multiple known td/path scopes belong to the same session, pass them through one session-preflight call with repeated --td-id/--path arguments instead of launching parallel lower-level claim commands.

./repo-git is the guarded git lane for agent worktree/index mutations. It must pass read-only git commands through and block destructive worktree/index commands when the tree is dirty unless a human deliberately uses the explicit unsafe override.
The default compact payload must stay small enough for every autonomous seed to read at turn start without flooding context.
Preflight closeout commands are advisory but should be copied into host instructions and final-turn discipline until real Codex hooks replace the manual lifecycle.

Agent-facing help and diagnostic surfaces for session-finalize must include a copyable append-exempt finalizer template for commit-only or projection-only closeouts, including --read-receipt-id, --append-exempt-reason, --append-exempt-ref, and the rule that this finalizes the session and must not be followed by bare session-finalize.

session-finalize is the normal one-command closeout path after a touched session has written its Work Ledger append. If touched work has no append, session-finalize blocks before ending the session and returns the append-before-finalize repair route. --allow-missing-append is a diagnostic escape hatch that preserves the old stale-finalize path; --no-release-claims is diagnostic only and a finalized session with active claims is not considered cleanly closed unless that flag was intentionally used for runtime inspection.

same_session_mutation_ordering.purpose: Prevent agents from treating independent path scopes as permission to run concurrent writes against the same runtime_status session row.
applies_to: session-sweep when it can touch the same session
rules: Work Ledger lifecycle and claim commands that mutate one session are a single-writer sequence. Do not launch them in parallel for the same session.
Use session-preflight as the batch lane for known startup scope: repeated --path, repeated --td-id, and --write-profile expansion are serialized inside one command.
If new scopes are discovered after preflight, run each session-claim, session-claim-path, or session-release-claim command only after the prior command returns; finalize last.
Read-only commands such as mutation-check, session-status, and session-claims may be parallelized because they do not mutate the session row.
A future batch-claim command must preserve this contract by performing its internal writes serially and returning one ordered claim_summary.

shell_command_hygiene_contract.purpose: Keep agent-authored shell wrappers around Work Ledger lifecycle and claim commands from corrupting the shell environment or hiding coordination failures.
applies_to: manual or generated examples that wrap session-preflight, session-claim, session-claim-path, session-release-claim, or session-finalize in a shell loop
retry snippets used while setting up Work Ledger claims before source mutation
safe_next_command or owner_repair_command text when it includes shell variables or multi-command examples
rules: Do not use path as a shell variable name in Work Ledger command snippets. In zsh, path is tied to PATH, so assigning path can clobber command lookup for dirname, basename, python, repo-python, and owner CLIs.
Use neutral variable names such as claim_path, owned_path, scope_path, or claim_file in examples and ad hoc loops, and quote every expansion.

Prefer a single session-preflight call with repeated --path / --td-id arguments when the owner CLI can express the startup scope, instead of piping Work Ledger output through a shell loop or extra interpreter.

If a loop is unavoidable, keep it small, serialize each Work Ledger mutation for the session, check each command exit status before continuing, and never mask a failed claim command behind a later loop iteration.

workitem_write_admission_contract.purpose: Host-neutral pre-edit gate for write-capable agents. It verifies the WorkItem-first entry result has been bound to a live Work Ledger execution attempt before source mutation.
can_write_true_only_when: the WorkItem selector classifies the subject as agent-executable, or the caller explicitly declares an exact-subject override
a work_landing_attempt_binding_v0 exists for the subject/session
the Work Ledger session is active
the attempt td thread is open
the WorkItem claim, td claim, and every owned path claim are active for the same session
no overlapping active claim collision exists
kernel_phase_required is false and active_phase_consulted is false
workitem_write_admission_contract.false_result_contract: A false admission result must include reason, missing_fields, claim_collisions, and required_next_command so an agent can repair the envelope instead of editing from chat memory.
non_goals: admission-check does not create sessions, claims, td threads, Task Ledger events, commits, receipts, or projections
admission-check does not make kernel or phase state execution authority
admission-check does not grant durable proof status to external/signoff work without an explicit subject override plus valid claims

work_landing_subject_authority_contract.purpose: Separate Task Ledger event authority from deterministic projection/card visibility when Work Landing binds a subject before source mutation.
applies_to: Task Ledger quick-capture rows used as Work Landing subjects
closeout traces where an authority-visible capture is not yet projection-visible
rules: A WorkItem exists for Work Landing subject binding when either the deterministic Task Ledger projection contains the row or state/task_ledger/events.jsonl contains a work_item.* event for the subject id.
Projection/card visibility is a discoverability and settlement state, not the same question as event authority existence.
Do not require a projection rebuild merely to bind a landing attempt for a freshly authority-visible capture when the source edit can stay scoped to exact owned paths.
Use --explicit-subject-override only for intentionally non-Task-Ledger subjects or operator/external subjects; do not use it to compensate for an authority-visible WorkItem whose projection is stale.
After landing, settle Task Ledger projections through the owner rebuild or generated-state drainer lane before relying on card visibility, execution-menu status, or selected WorkItem card output.
A stale projection is still a closeout fact: record projection_visible=false or projection_status=stale_until_rebuild when it matters, rather than claiming the WorkItem card is visible.
work_landing_subject_authority_contract.overfit_guard: The rule is generic over fresh captures, shaped WorkItems, and external trace closeouts; product, phase, and cap names in one transcript are examples only.

claim_card_first_contract.purpose: Make compact active claim cards the first evidence surface for trace-summary, experience-friction, and landing-preflight claim contention so agents do not mine raw transcripts or improvise around active owners.
applies_when: trace_improvement_surface or session-diagnostics rows identify experience_friction, ledger_claim_contention, or work_ledger_claim_collision
session-preflight, mission_transaction_preflight, scoped_commit, or mutation-check reports active claim pressure
an agent needs to decide whether a claim is its own session, a live peer, a stale/orphaned session, or only advisory observed-path overlap
rules: Read the cards-only claim surface before opening full session rows, rollout traces, or raw transcripts.
Use active claim cards as the first evidence for session_id, actor, phase, scope_kind, scope_id/path/td_id, lease freshness, release/expiry state, and collision status.

session-claims --refresh and --session-summary must derive effective_active_sessions from the same predicate as cohort_overview, including passive external-observed demotion, so claim cards and overview cards do not report conflicting concurrency pressure.

Any generated session_workflow_messages.query_commands.query_claim_owner command must include session-claims --refresh so an owner-discovery handoff reads the current active-claims snapshot before a peer decides that a path is blocked, released, or claimable.

Unclaimed touched work must be surfaced with a session_coordination_packet that includes read-only query commands, a refreshed claim-owner query, and an owner_repair_command gated to the touched-session owner or an explicit coordinator.
Claim summary grouping must recognize canonical scope_kind values path, td_id, and work_item_id; legacy aliases may remain supported but must not turn work-item or td claims into other_claim_count.

If a landing or mutation preflight returns a sessionless_claim_identity_hint or candidate --session-id, rerun the check bound to that session id before treating the claim as an external blocker or switching to broad/full-path review.

When a same-session touched WorkItem id is a bridge alias and the session holds an active canonical child claim with the touched id plus an underscore-delimited suffix, unclaimed-touched detection treats that alias as owner-covered. This is not arbitrary prefix equivalence and never covers cross-session WorkItems.

Treat heartbeat gaps on other live sessions as read-only authority boundaries: use the session-status drilldown or read_only_alternative_command to classify the owner, and do not heartbeat, finalize, release, or bypass another session's claims.
Open --full claim rows, session-status --full, rollout activity, or host transcripts only when cards and the session-bound preflight cannot decide ownership, scope overlap, or lease freshness.
When claim contention blocks the primary mutation lane, route through blocked_primary_ambition_preservation rather than making a smaller unrelated hygiene edit.
claim_card_first_contract.raw_body_policy: Raw transcripts and full session bodies are diagnostic escape hatches. They are not the first-contact repair surface for claim contention when compact claim cards can classify the blocker.

claim_contract.purpose: Forward-looking lease on a td_* or repo-relative path for multi-agent coordination BEFORE mutation, complementing the post-hoc touched_td_ids signal. Claims are runtime-only coordination state, never persisted to the append-only JSONL substrate.
claim_contract.location: Per-session `claims` array inside state/work_ledger/runtime_status.json. Also surfaced in cohort_overview.active_claims and cohort_overview.contention.claim_collisions.

claim_row_shape.scope_id: target td_*, normalized repo-relative path, or Task Ledger WorkItem id
claim_row_shape.td_id: target thread id when scope_kind=td_id; retained for backwards compatibility
claim_row_shape.leased_until: iso 8601 utc; set at claim time as now + lease_minutes, clamped to ACTIVE_CLAIM_LEASE_MAX (12h)
claim_row_shape.released_at: iso 8601 utc when released explicitly; null while active
claim_row_shape.expired_at: iso 8601 utc when sweep marks the lease past; null while active or released; preserved even after expiry
claim_row_shape.require_exclusive: bool — when true, the claim is refused at record time if another active session holds an unexpired overlapping claim on the same scope

lifecycle_states.active: released_at == null AND expired_at == null AND leased_until > now
lifecycle_states.expired: released_at == null AND expired_at != null (sweep stamped expired_at = leased_until)

claim_contract.collision_policy: Soft by default: `session-claim` and `session-claim-path` record the claim even when a collision exists and return a `claim_collisions` list so the calling agent can decide. With --require-exclusive the claim is refused when a collision exists and the runtime is not mutated. td_* collisions are exact-match; path collisions treat a directory claim and any child path claim as overlapping. Expired or released claims never count as collisions. Mission landing decisions must evaluate same-mission td/path claim collisions before shared-index or private-index review lanes, so an active collision cannot be downgraded to a staged-index review.
claim_contract.unclaimed_touch_policy: If an effective-active session marks session-activity with a td_id or WorkItem id but has no live claim covering that subject, cohort_overview must surface the session under contention.unclaimed_touched_sessions, increment counts.unclaimed_touched_sessions, emit the unclaimed_touched_work signal, and recommend claiming or finalizing before mutation proceeds. Same-session canonical WorkItem child claims cover bridge aliases only when the claimed WorkItem id starts with the touched alias plus an underscore delimiter; plain string-prefix matches do not count. Seed-speed unclaimed-touched rows must include an owner-visible coordination packet with read-only drilldown, refreshed claim-owner query, and an owner_repair_command; non-owner consumers inspect or coordinate rather than mutating through the command directly.
bi_temporal_invariants: Claims are never deleted — released_at / expired_at are always stamped rather than removing the row.
A crashed holder leaves a visible `was-claimed-but-never-released` trail rather than silently disappearing.
Sweep is idempotent: re-running sweep_expired_claims or sweep_orphan_sessions yields the same state.
claim_contract.annex_provenance: Pattern transferred 2026-04-21 from annexes/graphiti (bi-temporal edge lifecycle, n004) and annexes/continuous-claude-v3 (lease-based coordination checkpoint). No substrate imported; shape translated into runtime_status.json.

sweep_policy.rule: Two thresholds separate display hygiene from cleanup. ACTIVE_SESSION_ORPHAN_AFTER (4h) hides a session from live contention math in cohort_overview. ACTIVE_SESSION_ORPHAN_SWEEP_AFTER (24h) triggers auto-finalization on the next bootstrap_session and/or on explicit session-sweep runs. Sweep sets ended_at, end_action=auto_orphan_sweep, and releases all active claims (release_reason=session_auto_swept). Session rows, touched_td_ids, stale flags, and claim history are preserved for audit.
triggers: session-sweep CLI is SAFE-registerable for the reactions engine on future signal digests

recent_sweep_events_shape.details: list of compact per-item rows (session_id, claim_id, td_id, reasons)

write_invariants: No direct hand-editing of work_ledger.jsonl in normal operation; use tools/meta/factory/work_ledger.py.
Raw substrate is append-only; never mutate prior rows in place.
todo_close and todo_reopen preserve reversal history instead of flattening state.
todo_supersede is explicit and links old thread to successor thread.
Mutating writes require a valid read_receipt_id issued by the runtime helper.

runtime_enforcement.host_runtime_cli: Non-Claude hosts may explicitly call `session-preflight` for the normal autonomous-seed entry path, or the lower-level `session-bootstrap`, `session-activity`, `session-heartbeat`, `session-finalize`, `session-status --seed-speed`, `session-status --overview`, `session-import-codex`, `session-yield-request`, `session-yield-result`, `session-yield-control`, `session-claim`, `session-claim-path`, `session-release-claim`, and `session-sweep` commands when they need individual lifecycle steps. Participating writable sessions should call `session-heartbeat` at long pass start, plan pivot, before validation, and closeout; this publishes bounded public now/done coordination text only, not transcript summaries or durable progress. `session-yield-request --coordination-brief` emits a paste-ready sibling-thread request bound to the same `syr_*` receipt. `session-activity`, `session-heartbeat`, `session-finalize`, and `session-status` are compact by default; use `--full` only when debugging `state/work_ledger/runtime_status.json` itself. `session-finalize` blocks before ending a touched session that has no Work Ledger append, and releases active claims by default after the append exists; use `--allow-missing-append` only for intentional stale-finalize diagnostics and `--no-release-claims` only for diagnostic cases that intentionally preserve unreleased claim state.
runtime_enforcement.session_start: Emit a bounded open slice and mint a read_receipt_id.
runtime_enforcement.session_end: If work was touched and the obligation was not cleared by a ledger append, block session-finalize before ending the session. Only explicit --allow-missing-append diagnostic finalization marks the session stale in runtime_status.json.
runtime_enforcement.reaction_expectation: Reactions consume runtime_status.json through the work_ledger_status source kind and may trigger deterministic projection refreshes. They never invent missing progress rows.

v1_non_goals: No vector store or graph database backing store.
No automatic transcript summarization into ledger events or pass heartbeat lines.
No separate vector or graph-backed work-memory item store; work_memory_items are deterministic projection rows.
No automatic Codex or Cursor hook enforcement until their host lifecycle surfaces exist; host-neutral CLI lifecycle commands are the interim contract.
